title: Fix bottlenecks with controlled parallelism, capacity policy and safe workspace concurrency
core_principle: Do not simply increase parallelism. Enforce bounded, measurable and policy-approved concurrency across Hub, Worker, Ollama and output workspaces.

observed: Ollama is not explicitly configured for parallel requests.
Global sgpt_lock serializes central SGPT subprocess calls.
Autopilot effective concurrency can default to 1 due to security_policy.max_concurrency_cap fallback.
Hub dispatch to Worker propose endpoint is synchronous and blocks during LLM calls.
Worker health may be confused with worker busy state.
Strategy chaining can trigger multiple sequential LLM calls per task.
Increasing parallelism interacts dangerously with shared output_dir writes.
risk: Naively setting max_concurrency=8 can overload VRAM, create failed LLM calls and introduce workspace race conditions.
Removing sgpt_lock without replacement can create CLI/cache/session side effects.
Shorter health intervals do not fix a single-threaded blocked worker.
Parallel writes to the same output_dir can corrupt generated artifacts.

references.reason: primary analysis document to update and keep aligned with implementation
global sgpt_lock currently serializes SGPT subprocess calls
effective concurrency is capped by security_policy.max_concurrency_cap
synchronous Hub to Worker propose dispatch
workspace/output_dir handling affected by parallel execution
file action pack path checks and workspace writes affected by parallel execution
strategy chaining can multiply LLM calls

milestones.title: Make current bottlenecks measurable
milestones.goal: Before increasing concurrency, expose enough metrics to know where time is actually lost.

milestones.title: Enable bounded Ollama and Autopilot concurrency
milestones.goal: Allow controlled parallelism without bypassing security policy or exceeding runtime capacity.

milestones.title: Replace global LLM locks with backend-specific semaphores
milestones.goal: Remove unnecessary serialization while preserving deterministic resource control.

milestones.title: Separate worker busy, degraded and offline states
milestones.goal: Prevent long LLM work from being misinterpreted as worker failure.

milestones.title: Protect shared output_dir from parallel write races
milestones.goal: Ensure higher concurrency does not corrupt project output.

milestones.title: Prepare async dispatch architecture
milestones.goal: Move toward ACK + callback dispatch only after bounded concurrency and metrics are stable.

tasks.title: Update BOTTLENECKS_ANALYSIS.md with corrected concurrency semantics
tasks.description: Correct the statement that max_concurrency simply defaults to 2. The current effective concurrency is bounded by security_policy.max_concurrency_cap and defaults to 1 when the cap is missing.
acceptance_criteria: Document explicitly states that max_concurrency_cap fallback can force effective concurrency to 1.
Document warns against blindly setting max_concurrency=8.
Document describes controlled parallelism using requested concurrency, policy cap, worker count and Ollama capacity.
Document includes a clear formula or pseudocode for effective concurrency.

tasks.title: Add Ollama parallelism environment configuration
tasks.description: Add explicit Ollama runtime knobs with conservative defaults.
required_changes: Optionally add OLLAMA_NUM_CTX via .env if not already consistently configured.
acceptance_criteria: docker-compose exposes OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL as configurable environment variable.
Default does not exceed 2 until measured.
.env.example documents recommended values for 8GB, 10GB, 16GB and 24GB VRAM classes if known.
Changing OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL does not require code changes.
Documentation warns that higher values increase VRAM and KV-cache usage.

tasks.title: Introduce runtime capacity model
tasks.description: Create explicit runtime capacity settings used to calculate effective concurrency.
acceptance_criteria: Effective concurrency is computed from requested concurrency, security cap, online workers and runtime capacity.
Missing config results in conservative concurrency, not implicit unlimited parallelism.
Computed effective concurrency is visible in debug/status output.
Unit tests cover missing cap, explicit cap, low worker count and low Ollama capacity.
Security policy remains the upper bound even if runtime capacity is higher.

tasks.title: Add bottleneck metrics for queue, dispatch, worker and LLM phases
tasks.description: Expose timing metrics before and after concurrency changes.
acceptance_criteria: Each task trace includes queue wait, dispatch start, propose start/end and execute start/end timestamps.
LLM calls report duration and backend/provider.
Metrics can be shown in existing status/debug endpoint or a new endpoint.
Metrics collection does not change task behavior.
At least one integration test verifies metrics are populated for a simple task.

tasks.title: Add controlled benchmark script for concurrency settings
tasks.description: Create repeatable benchmark for OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL and autopilot concurrency.
acceptance_criteria: Benchmark creates a fixed set of comparable tasks.
Benchmark records total runtime, success count, failure count and p50/p95 LLM duration.
Benchmark records GPU/VRAM snapshot if nvidia-smi is available.
Benchmark output is JSON and human-readable markdown summary.
Benchmark can run locally without external cloud APIs.

tasks.title: Replace global sgpt_lock with backend-specific semaphores
tasks.description: Replace the global threading.Lock with configurable semaphores per CLI/backend.
acceptance_criteria: SGPT calls no longer use a global exclusive Lock.
Parallelism is bounded by a Semaphore, not unlimited.
Default remains conservative.
Timeout behavior remains unchanged or improves.
Concurrent SGPT unit test proves two allowed calls can overlap when semaphore permits.
Semaphore exhaustion is observable via metric or debug log.
Errors fail closed and do not leak permits.

tasks.title: Add worker busy/degraded/offline state distinction
tasks.description: Do not treat long-running LLM work as offline if the worker is alive but busy.
acceptance_criteria: Health endpoint can respond while a long LLM call is running, or worker state explicitly reports busy.
Dispatcher does not send unlimited new work to busy workers.
Offline state is reserved for unreachable workers or repeated hard failures.
Health timeout and interval are configurable.
Tests simulate long-running propose and verify worker is not incorrectly marked offline.

tasks.title: Verify Worker HTTP server concurrency model
tasks.description: Determine whether Worker Flask server can handle /health while /step/propose is running.
acceptance_criteria: Document current server mode: Flask dev server, threaded, gunicorn, process count, thread count.
A test or script proves /health latency during an artificial long propose call.
If single-threaded, add explicit task to move to threaded/multi-worker serving.
Health behavior is documented in BOTTLENECKS_ANALYSIS.md.

tasks.title: Introduce proposal budget per task
tasks.description: Prevent sequential strategy chaining from causing unbounded multi-call LLM latency.
acceptance_criteria: A task has a total proposal timeout, not only per-strategy timeout.
Strategy attempts stop when max_llm_calls is reached.
Failure reason reports budget_exhausted when applicable.
Existing successful single-strategy proposals still work.
Tests cover max_total_seconds, max_llm_calls and max_strategy_attempts.

tasks.title: Make Autopilot wakeups event-aware before adding Redis
tasks.description: Trigger wake() on relevant state changes to reduce polling delay without adding a full event bus yet.
acceptance_criteria: Autopilot wakes immediately after task creation.
Autopilot wakes after worker result completion.
Autopilot wakes after worker registration/status recovery.
Polling interval can stay conservative because wakeups handle fast path.
Tests verify wake() is called for at least task_created and task_completed.

tasks.title: Protect shared output_dir from parallel write conflicts
tasks.description: Define and enforce how multiple tasks may write to the same output_dir.
acceptance_criteria: Default mode prevents two active tasks from writing to the same output_dir.
Lock is released on task completion, failure or timeout.
Stale locks are recoverable with explicit stale threshold.
Conflict produces clear error: output_dir_busy or workspace_write_conflict.
Tests cover same output_dir conflict, different output_dir success and stale lock recovery.
unsafe_shared is not default and requires explicit config.

tasks.title: Align output_dir path policy with concurrency model
tasks.description: Integrate previously proposed deterministic output_dir validation with concurrency-safe write ownership.
acceptance_criteria: requested output_dir is validated before use.
Only approved resolved workspace dirs are lockable/writable.
Path traversal and symlink escape are rejected.
Shared output_dir locking uses the resolved canonical path.
File access checks use commonpath-style containment, not string startswith.

tasks.title: Design async dispatch protocol
tasks.description: Specify ACK + result callback protocol before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Protocol defines request, ack, result and failure payloads.
Protocol defines idempotency keys.
Protocol defines retry and stale recovery.
Protocol defines how worker capacity is reserved and released.
Protocol defines security requirements for callback authentication.

tasks.title: Implement async propose dispatch behind feature flag
tasks.description: Add asynchronous dispatch only after metrics and bounded concurrency are stable.
acceptance_criteria: Default remains existing sync dispatch until flag is enabled.
Worker immediately ACKs accepted propose work.
Worker later submits result to Hub or Hub polls result endpoint.
Hub recovers stale proposing tasks.
Feature can be rolled back without DB migration loss.
Integration test covers async success and async worker failure.

tasks.title: Evaluate Redis/Event-Bus only after wake/callback baseline
tasks.description: Avoid adding Redis as first response. Evaluate after simple wake and async callback are measured.
acceptance_criteria: Document states which bottleneck Redis would solve.
Document states which bottlenecks Redis would not solve.
Redis is not required for P0/P1 performance fixes.
Decision includes operational cost, failure modes and local-first impact.

tasks.title: Add regression tests for effective concurrency calculation
test_cases: missing max_concurrency_cap defaults to conservative value
requested concurrency above cap is capped
worker count below cap limits effective concurrency
ollama capacity below requested limits effective concurrency
security cap is always upper bound
acceptance_criteria: Tests fail if effective concurrency ignores security cap.
Tests cover explicit cap 1, 2, 4 and 8.
Tests cover zero/invalid values and fail closed.

tasks.title: Add regression tests for SGPT semaphore release
acceptance_criteria: Semaphore permit is released after successful subprocess.
Semaphore permit is released after TimeoutExpired.
Semaphore permit is released after unexpected exception.
Configured parallelism of 1 preserves old serial behavior.
Configured parallelism of 2 allows two overlapping mocked calls.

tasks.title: Add worker health under load test
acceptance_criteria: Start or mock a long-running propose request.
Call /health while propose is running.
Assert /health returns within configured timeout or reports busy state.
Assert worker is not marked offline solely due to long propose.

tasks.title: Add shared output_dir conflict tests
acceptance_criteria: Two tasks targeting same output_dir cannot write concurrently in default mode.
Two tasks targeting different output_dirs can proceed concurrently.
Stale output_dir lock can be recovered deterministically.
Resolved canonical path is used for lock identity.
Symlink-equivalent paths map to same canonical lock target.

tasks.title: Document controlled parallelism model
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain difference between requested concurrency, effective concurrency, worker capacity and Ollama capacity.
Docs warn against blindly setting max_concurrency=8.
Docs include recommended starting values.
Docs explain how to benchmark and roll back.
Docs mention shared output_dir write policy.

definition_of_done: Ollama parallelism is configurable and benchmarked.
Effective Autopilot concurrency is computed from policy and capacity, not guessed.
Global SGPT lock is replaced by safe bounded semaphores.
Worker health distinguishes busy from offline.
Task proposal has bounded strategy/LLM budget.
Output directories are protected against parallel write conflicts.
Metrics show where time is spent before and after changes.
Async dispatch is designed and feature-flagged, not forced prematurely.
Documentation reflects controlled parallelism and safety constraints.
suggested_commits: docs: refine bottleneck analysis with controlled parallelism model
config: expose ollama parallelism and runtime capacity settings
fix: compute effective autopilot concurrency from policy and runtime capacity
perf: replace global sgpt lock with bounded backend semaphores
fix: distinguish worker busy state from offline health failures
fix: protect shared output dirs from parallel write conflicts
docs: add performance benchmark and rollback guidance
